reasoning: The theorem statement in the ORIGINAL is byte-for-byte identical to the CORRECTED version. Both assert: given a family `u : ι → Submodule F V`, there exists a `Submodule F V` whose carrier equals the set intersection `⋂ i, (u i).carrier`. This is exactly the informal claim that the intersection of any collection of subspaces is itself a subspace. The only difference between the two files is the extra `InnerProductSpace` in `open scoped ...` at the top of the original, which is unused and has no semantic effect on the theorem statement. Therefore the original is faithful and provably true, with no error.
